title: eriogonum effusum subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum effusum subsp. typicum, commonly known as desert buckwheat, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It is a shrub-like perennial with small white or pink flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, likely due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Desert buckwheat can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional herbal remedies for various ailments.
